title: nymphaea alba subsp. occidentalis
intro: Nymphaea alba subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western white water lily, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the genus Nymphaea and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Nymphaea alba subsp. occidentalis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support skin health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
